exercises.prompt: In basic conditional GANs, we guide the generator with a label. What do we use to guide it when we want to transform a sketch into a photo?
exercises.explain: Conditional GANs can use simple labels, but to transform detailed pictures, we need to guide them with a whole input image!

cards.title: From Sketch to Photo
cards.body: Imagine drawing a simple outline of a cat and having it instantly turn into a fluffy, photorealistic kitten. That is the magic of Pix2Pix, which takes whole images as guides!

cards.title: What is Pix2Pix?
cards.body: Pix2Pix is a type of Conditional GAN where the 'condition' is a complete input image (like a sketch or a map) instead of just a simple text category or number. This guides the generator at a precise pixel level.
Think of standard cGANs as a painter told to paint 'a dog'. But Pix2Pix is like a painter given a black-and-white coloring book page and told to color it in exactly within the lines!
cards.title: The Coloring Book
Maps to Satellites
cards.body: Pix2Pix can take a simple black-and-white street map layout and generate a realistic satellite photo of that exact neighborhood, matching buildings to their outlined blocks.

cards.title: Not Just for Sketches
cards.body: You might think Pix2Pix only works for drawing. Actually, it works for any image-to-image task, like turning daytime photos into night, or colorizing historic black-and-white photos!

right.text: Generates a specific cat matching the exact lines of your sketch

left.text: Generates a generic random cat from the category 'cat'

cards.title: Label vs. Image Guidance
cards.body: See how conditioning on a whole image changes the output detail compared to using a simple label.

exercises.prompt: How does Pix2Pix use image conditioning?

options.text: It translates an input image into a new output image by using the entire input image as a condition.
It only uses word labels to generate random images from scratch.
It deletes pixels from an image to make it smaller.

exercises.explain: Pix2Pix takes a full image (like a sketch) as an input condition to generate a corresponding output image (like a photo).

exercises.prompt: True or False: In Pix2Pix, the generator receives a complete image as its guiding condition.
exercises.explain: True! Pix2Pix is designed for image-to-image translation, meaning both the input condition and the output are full images.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Pix2Pix guides the generator by feeding a whole image as the ________ instead of a class label.
exercises.sentence: Pix2Pix guides the generator by feeding a whole image as the [blank] instead of a class label.
exercises.explain: By feeding the entire image as the condition, the generator knows exactly where to place colors, textures, and shapes.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents an image generated using a Pix2Pix style model?

options.text: A photo of a handbag taken with a smartphone camera
A realistic handbag image generated from a hand-drawn sketch outline

exercises.explain: The handbag photo created directly from a line drawing is generated using Pix2Pix's image-to-image translation.

exercises.prompt: What is a key requirement for training a standard Pix2Pix model?

options.text: It requires paired training images, showing the input and the target side-by-side.
It does not use a discriminator at all.
It only works with text descriptions.

exercises.explain: Pix2Pix is a supervised framework, meaning it requires pairs of matching images (like a sketch and its corresponding real photo) during training. Next, we will explore CycleGAN, which removes this pair requirement!

concept: Pix2Pix extends cGAN by feeding a whole input image as the condition, instead of a simple class label vector, guiding the generator at pixel level.
title: Conditioning on a Whole Image
